objection: The apparatus certifies every PAIRWISE verdict to the hilt but never once evaluates the thing it actually ships — the accumulated champion — and two compounding failures live in that gap. (1) Path dependence. A king-of-the-hill ladder is intrinsically order-sensitive, and the RUNNING MERGE amplifies it rather than averaging it out: whatever is grafted at rung 2 rewrites the prose that every later rung scores against, so the final artifact is a function of the seeding ORDER, not of the eight lineages' intrinsic merits. The incumbent-holds tie-break makes this strictly worse — it hands a structural edge to whoever happened to sit in the champion seat first, so near-tie rungs systematically resolve toward early-seeded material. The claim 'the champion is the accumulation of hardening drawn from all eight lineages' is really 'the accumulation along ONE walk of a path-dependent tree.' (2) The merge is the one generative, value-delivering act and simultaneously the LEAST verified one. Ordering+quotation+retry harden (a)-(d), and the margin-gated swapped second judge calibrates the VERDICT — but none of that touches (e). The graft that can silently corrupt the champion gets only an escape hatch that PERFORMS the edit; nothing ever checks that the edit failed to regress a graft merged three rungs earlier or to break global coherence. Local 'non-contradictory' is asserted against current prose by the same judge the schema pressures to find something salvageable; it is never checked against the whole. Net: you can win all eight rungs by clearing margins and still ship a chimera that is worse than the best single original — and the loop cannot detect it, because the load-bearing claim ('strictly better than the single best original') is the one quantity the system never measures. The design rigorously calibrates the selector and leaves the synthesizer unaudited.
riskiestAssumption: That 'mutually-compatible mechanism' is both reliably DECIDABLE by one judge and COMPOSABLE across rungs. The design assumes (i) a single reasoner can correctly certify that grafting the loser's mechanism is genuinely additive and non-contradictory to the champion, and (ii) that this property is monotonic — a graft certified compatible at rung 3 stays compatible after rungs 4-8 add more material. If compatibility is non-monotonic — if a later graft can retroactively contradict an earlier one — then 'compounds every compatible fix it meets' is simply false: the merge silently accumulates latent contradictions that no step rechecks, late rungs judge an internally inconsistent champion, and the synthesis-engine premise (the design's entire reason to exist over a plain selection ladder) collapses. Everything novel here rides on compatibility being a stable, composable predicate; nothing in the design tests that it is.
amendedIdea: Run a king-of-the-hill LADDER whose advancing artifact is a RUNNING MERGE compounding every compatible fix — but make the synthesizer as audited as the selector and bound the path dependence the merge introduces. SEEDING: pre-screen the eight originals with one cheap Opus pass that names the strongest as champion-zero, so the incumbency advantage accrues to the a-priori-best rather than to a random first mover; freeze the single best original in RESERVE. Each rung is one Opus call returning the ordered, gated chain: (a) steelman_both; (b) scores, each criterion quoting its verbatim rubric line and citing the steelman; (c) winner, with explicit MARGIN and dominance assertion (orchestrator retries once if the named winner contradicts its own scores); (d) loser_salvage — the loser's single strongest valid OBJECTION plus its strongest mutually-compatible MECHANISM, or an explicit 'nothing compatible' flag; (e) carry_forward — a before/after/change MERGE grafting both into the champion. TWO additions close the gaps. First, COMPOSABLE COMPATIBILITY: the champion carries a GRAFT LEDGER (one line per accepted graft). Field (e) must assert the new graft's non-contradiction not against current prose alone but against each live ledger entry; a flagged conflict does not silently accumulate — it forces the existing escape-hatch edit call to RESOLVE it (keep the stronger graft, drop or reconcile the weaker, update the ledger). This turns 'compatible' from a one-shot local guess into a re-validated, composable predicate, directly answering the riskiest assumption: if compatibility is non-monotonic, the conflict surfaces and is adjudicated instead of festering. Second, the WHOLE-ARTIFACT AUDIT that the original lacked: any rung with a non-no-op merge ends by re-judging the just-merged champion against the PREVIOUS champion on the same rubric — if the merged version cannot match-or-beat its own immediate predecessor, the merge regressed something and the orchestrator ROLLS BACK to the predecessor while still recording the rung's winner (selection and synthesis stay decoupled: a bad merge costs the graft, not the verdict). After the final rung, one mandatory AUDIT RUNG pits the accumulated champion against the reserved best original as a real challenger; the champion must clear the calibrated noise-floor margin, or the system ships the best original — making the central claim ('better than the single best original') a measured gate, not an assertion. Calibration is unchanged where it worked: a rung auto-certifies only when the margin clears the noise-floor band (>~1 of 5 criterion-points); any verdict inside the band triggers one fresh position-SWAPPED judgment with the prior verdict hidden — agree certifies, disagree IS the variance and incumbency holds. PATH DEPENDENCE is bounded honestly rather than denied: the reserved-best audit caps downside (you can never finish worse than the strongest original), the pre-screen seeding removes random first-mover bias, and the swapped recheck controls position bias within a rung — but order across rungs still shapes WHICH compatible mechanisms compound, so the design claims only 'a champion provably no worse than the best original and hardened by every graft that survived re-validation,' not 'the unique global optimum.' Cost stays bounded: dominant rungs are one call; contested near-ties spend a second; a contested rung that also edits or a merge that fails its predecessor audit spends a third; the final reserved-best audit adds exactly one call to the whole run — still well under the bracket's six-per-debate. Certification, synthesis, AND regression-control, none traded for the others.
openRisks: Residual path dependence survives: the reserved-best audit guarantees a floor (never worse than the strongest original) but not a global optimum — a different seeding order could still compound a different, possibly better, set of mechanisms, and the design does not explore alternate orders to avoid combinatorial cost. The graft ledger re-validation still relies on the SAME single judge to detect cross-graft contradiction; it makes conflicts surfaceable but inherits that judge's blind spots, and it grows linearly so late rungs carry a heavier (though still single-call) check. The predecessor-audit and reserved-best-audit add judge calls that themselves sit on the noise floor, so a merge could be rolled back (or the best original shipped) on a coin-flip near-tie — incumbent-holds mitigates direction but not the underlying variance. Finally, the pre-screen that names champion-zero is itself an unverified single judgment seeding the whole ladder; if it mis-ranks the originals, the reserve and incumbency advantages are anchored to the wrong artifact, and only the final audit (one comparison) guards against it.
